content.text: I'll refactor the auth flow so sign-in lives in the shared useAuth hook instead of being hand-rolled in the component. Let me read the two files first.

content.content: 142 lines. Login.tsx hand-rolls fetch('/api/login') and writes the token to localStorage directly, then redirects.

content.text: As expected — the token handling should move into the hook. Checking the hook's current API.

content.content: Exposes { user, signOut } but no signIn yet. Good place to add it.

content.text: Adding signIn to the hook — it posts the credentials and persists the returned session.
Now Login.tsx just calls signIn() and reads error from the hook — dropping the manual fetch.

input.old_string:
const r = await fetch('/api/login', { method:'POST', body });
localStorage.setItem('token', (await r.json()).token);
input.new_string:
const { signIn, error } = useAuth();
await signIn(email, pw);

content.text: The API service needs to return the user alongside the token. I'll delegate that to a subagent so it can update the service and run its suite independently.

input.prompt: Update src/auth/service.ts to return { token, user } instead of just { token }, then run the auth test suite and report results.

content.content: Updated service.ts to return { token, user }. Ran npm test -- auth: 12 passed, 0 failed.

content.text: Subagent is green. Running the full auth suite once more from the main session to confirm nothing regressed.

content.content:
PASS src/auth/service.test.ts
  ✓ signs in a valid user (38ms)
  ✓ rejects a bad password (11ms)

12 passed, 0 failed

result: Auth refactor is complete. Sign-in now flows through the shared useAuth() hook: Login.tsx delegates to signIn() and reads error from the hook, and the manual fetch + localStorage write are gone. The auth service returns a consistent { token, user } shape and the suite is green (12 passed). One branch is ready to review — say the word and I'll wire the remember-me checkbox next.
